item.title: Unraveling the probiotic evidence: how Lactobacillus pentosus from fermented vegetables alleviates colitis by enlisting muribaculum to boost host phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis.
item.description: Food & function - Probiotic-based modulation of the intestinal microenvironment is a key therapeutic strategy for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Lactobacillus pentosus (Lbp), a dominant bacterium in fermented veg...

item.title: Current and emerging therapeutic strategies targeting adherent-invasive Escherichia coli in Crohn's disease: a review.
item.description: Gut pathogens - Review - Considerable evidence has accumulated over the past two decades demonstrating that Escherichia coli, specifically adherent-invasive E. coli, contributes to the pathogenesis of Crohn's disease. Adhe...

item.title: Molecular signatures of the gut microbiota that affect longevity.
item.description: Biochemical pharmacology - Review - The human colonic microbiota has been estimated to contain 38 trillion bacteria whereas the total human somatic cells constitute 30 trillion. The mutualistic relationship between host and microbiom...

item.title: Purine and pyrimidine-based bacterial cyclic dinucleotides egress the phagosome and activate the innate immune sensor STING.
item.description: Immunity - Toll-like receptors (TLRs) are considered general sensors of bacterial encounters. Here, we examined whether other pattern recognition receptors are commonly activated during bacterial infection. T...

item.title: Lactobacillus johnsonii mediates the protective effects of pristimerin against ulcerative colitis and concomitant liver injury through remodeling hepatic lipid metabolism via LXRα-SCD1 axis.

item.title: M2 macrophage-based biohybrid system regulates intestinal microbiota homeostasis and immunity for the treatment of inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Acta biomaterialia - The pathogenesis of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) involves a self-perpetuating cycle driven by oxidative stress, microbial dysbiosis, and immune dysregulation. Restoring intestinal microbiota ho...

item.title: Probiotic-Derived Vesicles Rectally Delivered via Thermo-Gelling Copolymer Promote Mucosal Healing and Reduce Inflammation in Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Advanced science (Weinheim, Baden-Wurttemberg, Germany) - Ulcerative colitis remains challenging to treat due to the need for localized control of inflammation, restoration of mucosal integrity, and avoidance of systemic toxicity. Here, we report a rectal...

item.title: Mesenchymal stem cells and their exosomes as novel modulators of gut microbiota: Therapeutic perspectives for liver diseases and inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Microbial pathogenesis - Review - Gastrointestinal (GI) diseases such as liver diseases and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) have become a global economic burden due to their high prevalence, poor prognosis, and inadequacy of exist...

item.title: Dietary patterns, gut microbiota, and gastrointestinal disorders: mechanistic insights into irritable bowel syndrome and inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Reviews on environmental health - Review - The gut microbiota plays a central role in maintaining gastrointestinal (GI) health through its metabolic, immune-modulating, and barrier-supporting functions. Diet is among the most influential mo...

item.title: Regulatory T cell immunosuppressive function requires chaperone-mediated autophagy.
item.description: Autophagy - Regulatory T cells (Tregs) are essential for maintaining immune tolerance. We recently identified chaperone-mediated autophagy (CMA), a selective lysosomal degradation pathway, as a critical regula...

item.title: Hollow Cu2O Nanozymes Enhance Probiotic Therapy for Colitis via Redox Homeostasis and TXNIP/NLRP3 Inflammasome Inhibition.

item.title: Antigen-restricted Candida albicans Th17 cells link oral-gut immunity and adopt pathogenic features during intestinal inflammation.
item.description: Immunity - The commensal yeast Candida albicans is a major inducer of human mucosal Th17 cells. How C. albicans drives Th17 cell responses at homeostasis, and whether such responses contribute to inflammatory...

item.title: Immunomodulatory biomaterials as a novel therapeutic platform for inflammatory bowel disease.

item.title: Harmonized metagenomic signatures of the gut microbiome reveal robust species, functions, and strain links to inflammatory bowel disease.

item.title: Chitosan-coated and sodium phytate-crosslinked porous starch microspheres for oral delivery of celastrol in colitis therapy.
item.description: International journal of biological macromolecules - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), particularly ulcerative colitis (UC), imposes a growing clinical burden worldwide. Celastrol (Cel), a potent anti-inflammatory triterpenoid, holds considerable pro...

item.title: Magnoflorine, a dietary alkaloid from Alpinia oxyphylla fruit, ameliorates colitis by restoring intestinal barrier and dual modulation of JAK/STAT3 pathway.

item.title: Towards standardized gut microbiota diagnostics: normobiosis beyond geographical borders.

item.title: Osbeckia opipara attenuates inflammation and reconstructs the intestinal barrier in ulcerative colitis by modulating the AHR/IL-22/STAT3 signaling axis.

item.title: Partial enteral nutrition combined with an exclusion diet promotes a healthy gut microbiome in patients with mild to moderately active ulcerative colitis: a quasi-experimental study.
item.description: Crohn's & colitis 360 - The therapeutic role of enteral nutrition and diet in patients with ulcerative colitis (UC) has not been adequately explored. We aimed to evaluate the effectiveness of partial enteral nutrition (PE...

item.title: Postbiotic Nagqu4580 Attenuates Ulcerative Colitis and Suppresses Ferroptosis in Association with the Microbiota-Tryptophan-AhR/Nrf2 Axis.
item.description: Nutrients - Background/Objectives: Ferroptosis, an iron-dependent cell death driven by lipid peroxidation, is implicated in the pathogenesis of ulcerative colitis (UC). Tryptophan metabolism and its interactio...

item.title: Impact of Menopause and Clinical Considerations in Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Gastroenterology & hepatology - Menopause is marked by a natural decline in estrogen and progesterone that alters gut barrier integrity, immune regulation, and systemic inflammation. In women with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD)...
